instructions:
EN Diagram is a structural verification engine for concurrent systems. No AI inside.

To describe a system, write one statement per line:
  actor do: action needs: input1, input2 yields: output1, output2 at: context

Every statement requires: actor, do:, needs:, and yields:. Shared names between yields and needs create connections automatically. Multi-word names work fine. Use commas to separate multiple inputs or outputs. at: scopes the action to a context (service, team, region, domain, layer — whatever boundary fits the system).

Six tools answer six questions from concurrency theory. Each answer includes who (actors) and where (locations) alongside the structural findings.

1. structure — what is this system?
2. invariant — what's always true?
3. live — can it deadlock? can entities overflow?
4. reachable — can X reach Y?
5. equivalent — are two systems the same?
6. compose — how do parts combine?

Every tool output contains node names, values, and structural properties that can be fed directly into other tools. Read the output. Find the surprising node, the unexpected value, the structural anomaly. Then pick the tool that investigates THAT specific finding.

Do not stop at one tool call. The first call reveals the structure — the real insight comes from the second, third, fourth call that digs into what the first one found.

Speak in the user's domain, not in graph theory. Only call render when the user explicitly asks to visualize.

tools.name: structure
tools.description: What is this system? Complete structural overview: shape (topology), stages with roles, bridge nodes, cycles, parallelism, critical path, dominator tree, min-cuts, subsystems, interface nodes. Includes actors (who does what, workload entropy) and locations (where work happens, boundary crossings). Optional: pass node for per-node centrality, detect_findings for structural pattern detection.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: EN source code, or path to .en/.txt file

parameters.name: node
parameters.description: Node name for centrality query

parameters.name: detect_findings
parameters.description: Set to 'true' to detect structural findings

tools.name: invariant
tools.description: What's always true? conservationLaws are weighted entity sums constant across all executions. sustainableCycles are action sequences that return the system to its starting state (T-invariants). depletableSets are entity groups where simultaneous depletion is irreversible. behavioral.deficiency 0 means structure fully determines dynamics. behavioral.isReversible and behavioral.hasUniqueEquilibrium describe convergence properties.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: EN source code, or path to .en/.txt file

parameters.name: rules
parameters.description: Structural rules to check, one per line

tools.name: live
tools.description: Can it deadlock? Can entities overflow? isStructurallyLive means every siphon contains a trap — no structural deadlock possible. uncoveredSiphons are entity groups that can drain permanently, with the actors and locations affected. isStructurallyBounded means no entity can accumulate without limit. unboundedCycles are action sequences that could cause overflow.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: EN source code, or path to .en/.txt file

tools.name: reachable
tools.description: Can X reach Y? Follows directed data flow first; falls back to undirected. Path shows each step with actor and location. locationCrossings counts boundary transitions. defense_nodes checks if guards cover all paths. coverage.fullCoverage false means unguarded routes exist.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: EN source code

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Starting node name

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Target node name

parameters.name: defense_nodes
parameters.description: Comma-separated guard nodes to check coverage

tools.name: equivalent
tools.description: Are two systems the same? Compare mode (source_a + source_b): shows structural differences, edit distance, and spectral equivalence — isCospectral true means identical structure despite different names. Evolve mode (source + patch): dry-run a change, shows diff plus new/lost bridge nodes. Prefix action name with - in patch to remove it.

parameters.name: source_a
parameters.description: EN source code or path to .en/.txt file for the first system

parameters.name: source_b
parameters.description: EN source code or path to .en/.txt file for the second system

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: EN source code for evolve mode

parameters.name: patch
parameters.description: EN patch for evolve mode

tools.name: compose
tools.description: How do parts combine? Merge mode (source_a + source_b + links): merge two systems by linking shared entities. Extract mode (source + subsystem): extract a subsystem as standalone EN with boundary inputs/outputs, actors, and locations.

parameters.name: source_a
parameters.description: EN source code or path to .en/.txt file for the first system

parameters.name: source_b
parameters.description: EN source code or path to .en/.txt file for the second system

parameters.name: links
parameters.description: Entity links e.g. 'a.node1=b.node2'

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: EN source code for extract mode

parameters.name: subsystem
parameters.description: Subsystem name for extract mode

tools.name: render
tools.description: SVG diagram. Only call when user explicitly asks to visualize.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: EN source code, or path to .en/.txt file

parameters.name: theme
parameters.description: Color theme: dark or light

parameters.name: quality
parameters.description: Output quality: small, mid, or max

parameters.name: view
parameters.description: View mode: flow (default), actors (swimlane by actor), locations (grouped by context), summary (one node per subsystem)

parameters.name: structure_layers
parameters.description: Bitmask for structure overlays. Bits: 1=subsystems, 2=pipelines, 4=cycles, 8=forks, 16=joins, 32=hubs. Default 63 (all on). Pass 0 to hide all.

parameters.name: output
parameters.description: File path to save the SVG
